raw_title: Toxicity and Moral Rhetoric in Video- and Text-Based Digital Platforms: A Multimethod Study of Political Talk Shows and News Articles in Taiwan
title_en: Toxicity and Moral Rhetoric in Video- and Text-Based Digital Platforms: A Multimethod Study of Political Talk Shows and News Articles in Taiwan
title_zh: Toxicity and Moral Rhetoric in Video- and Text-Based Digital Platforms: A Multimethod Study of Political Talk Shows and News Articles in Taiwan
plain_summary_en: This paper studies Toxicity and Moral Rhetoric in Video- and Text-Based Digital Platforms: A Multimethod Study of Political Talk Shows and News Articles in Taiwan. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Politics, Rhetoric, Political rhetoric, Political communication. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
plain_summary_zh: This paper studies Toxicity and Moral Rhetoric in Video- and Text-Based Digital Platforms: A Multimethod Study of Political Talk Shows and News Articles in Taiwan. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Politics, Rhetoric, Political rhetoric, Political communication. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.
research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Politics, Rhetoric, Political rhetoric, Political communication, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
research_direction_zh: The paper points to an active research direction around Politics, Rhetoric, Political rhetoric, Political communication, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
